title: Raising and custom exceptions

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Raising and custom exceptions; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: The Python Tutorial; Python 3.14; Tutorial chapters 7-8: input/output, files, JSON, exceptions, and cleanup; atomic topic: Raising and custom exceptions
sources.claim: A custom Python exception names a domain failure that callers can handle distinctly, normally inherits from Exception, and carries safe structured context without replacing ordinary result alternatives.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A custom Python exception names a domain failure that callers can handle distinctly, normally inherits from Exception, and carries safe structured context without replacing ordinary result alternatives.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A parse_order function raises InvalidOrderError with a safe field name and chains the underlying ValueError for diagnostics. Your goal is to design and test failure behavior for a small module API. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Function contracts and returns; Unicode text and formatting; Exception handling and propagation; Modules and imports. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: A custom Python exception names a domain failure that callers can handle distinctly, normally inherits from Exception, and carries safe structured context without replacing ordinary result alternatives. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Too many exception types burden callers, messages are not stable machine APIs, secrets must not enter attributes, exception chaining matters, and expected absence may be better represented explicitly. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Every validation branch deserves a separate custom exception, and callers should parse exception message text to decide recovery.

includes: Validate contracts and define domain exceptions only when callers benefit from the distinction.
Design and test failure behavior for a small module API.
excludes: Too many exception types burden callers, messages are not stable machine APIs, secrets must not enter attributes, exception chaining matters, and expected absence may be better represented explicitly.

outcomes: Design and test failure behavior for a small module API.

examples.title: Raising and custom exceptions: direct language case
examples.situation: A parse_order function raises InvalidOrderError with a safe field name and chains the underlying ValueError for diagnostics.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A parse_order function raises InvalidOrderError with a safe field name and chains the underlying ValueError for diagnostics.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Define a custom exception only for a useful caller-visible failure category, expose stable safe attributes, and preserve the original cause with chaining.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Define a custom exception only for a useful caller-visible failure category, expose stable safe attributes, and preserve the original cause with chaining.

examples.title: Raising and custom exceptions: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A lookup returns None for documented optional absence but raises RepositoryUnavailable when it cannot determine whether data exists.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A lookup returns None for documented optional absence but raises RepositoryUnavailable when it cannot determine whether data exists.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Too many exception types burden callers, messages are not stable machine APIs, secrets must not enter attributes, exception chaining matters, and expected absence may be better represented explicitly.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Every validation branch deserves a separate custom exception, and callers should parse exception message text to decide recovery.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Design and test failure behavior for a small module API.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Every validation branch deserves a separate custom exception, and callers should parse exception message text to decide recovery.
misconceptions.correction: Define a custom exception only for a useful caller-visible failure category, expose stable safe attributes, and preserve the original cause with chaining.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: Every validation branch deserves a separate custom exception, and callers should parse exception message text to decide recovery.

analogy.example: a records office that escalates named failures, validates decoded forms, and returns borrowed resources on every exit can introduce Raising and custom exceptions, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: exceptions, custom failure categories, files, JSON, and contexts
limitations: exception chaining, suppression, path resolution, encoding, partial writes, and protocol methods are software rules
